hero.title: Research Feed
hero.description: Discover the latest from arXiv, HuggingFace, GitHub, WeChat 公众号, X, and Xiaohongshu — automatically scored by relevance, recency, popularity, and quality.

status.noActiveSources: No active sources selected. Toggle sources above to see results.
status.noResultsHint: Complete the setup above, then click "Start Search"

steps: Click "Settings" to configure your research domains and keywords
Add arXiv categories (e.g. cs.AI, cs.LG) to filter relevant papers
Adjust "Results to show" and "Max results to scan" as needed
Click "Start Search" to fetch the latest papers
Open Settings to choose which HF Hub modes to fetch (Daily Papers, Models, Datasets, Spaces)
Optionally set HF_TOKEN in your environment for higher rate limits
Add research domains in Settings to filter by your interests (optional — without domains, you see trending entries)
Click "Start Search" to fetch the latest items
Open Settings to configure your research domains and (optionally) a language filter
Optionally set GITHUB_TOKEN in your environment for higher rate limits (10/min unauth → 30/min auth)
Choose a trending time window (daily / weekly / monthly)
Click "Start Search" to fetch trending and recently created repositories
github.note: Without GITHUB_TOKEN, the GitHub Search API is rate-limited to 10 requests/minute.

steps: WeChat 公众号 articles are fetched via RSSHub — the public instance works out of the box
Open Settings → paste a list of 公众号 routes (one per line, e.g. wechat/ce/huxiu_com)
If the public RSSHub is rate-limited, point the instance URL at your own self-hosted RSSHub
Optionally add research domains in Settings to filter articles by your interests
Click "Start Search" to fetch the latest articles
wechat.note: Public rsshub.app instances may be rate-limited. For reliable use, run RSSHub yourself: docker run -d -p 1200:1200 diygod/rsshub

steps: Make sure you are logged into X (Twitter) in your browser (Chrome, Arc, Edge, or Firefox)
twitter-cli will automatically extract your browser cookies — no API key needed
Click "Settings" to configure search queries and tracked accounts
Click "Start Search" to fetch the latest tweets
x.note: If you haven't installed twitter-cli yet, run: uv tool install twitter-cli

steps: If you use browser cookies, make sure a browser on the machine running the dr-claw service is logged into xiaohongshu.com
For remote deployments, prefer "Login via QR Code"
Configure your search keywords in Settings
Click "Start Search" to fetch the latest posts
xiaohongshu.note: If you haven't installed xiaohongshu-cli yet, run: uv tool install xiaohongshu-cli

settings.xAuthDescription: twitter-cli automatically extracts cookies from your browser (Chrome, Arc, Edge, Firefox). Make sure you are logged into X/Twitter in your browser. No API key needed.
settings.githubAuthDescription: GitHub API works without a token, but is rate-limited to 10 search requests/minute. Paste a token below to raise it to 30/minute. The token only needs the public_repo (read) scope. Stored in your account's credential store; takes precedence over the GITHUB_TOKEN env var.
settings.hfAuthDescription: HuggingFace endpoints used here are public. Setting an HF token is optional and only raises rate limits / unlocks gated repos. Token is stored in your account's credential store and overrides the HF_TOKEN env var.
settings.tokenHelp: Stored securely in your account's credential store. Leave blank to fall back to the environment variable.
settings.tokenSavedPlaceholder: Token already saved — paste to replace
settings.tokenPendingClear: Will be removed from the credential store when you save.
settings.hfModesHelp: Choose which HuggingFace Hub surfaces to include in this feed.
settings.githubIncludeTrendingHelp: When on, GitHub Trending repos are included alongside repos that match your research domains.
settings.wechatRsshubDescription: WeChat 公众号 articles are fetched via RSSHub. The public instance works for casual use; self-host for reliability.
settings.wechatInstanceHelp: Default: https://rsshub.app. Point this at your own RSSHub if the public instance is rate-limited.
settings.wechatAccessKeyPlaceholder: Optional access key for private RSSHub instances
settings.wechatAccountsHelp: One route per line. Each entry can be a relative RSSHub path (wechat/ce/<id>), a full URL, or just the bare ID (auto-prefixed with wechat/ce/).
settings.wechatAccountsFormat: Tip: most stable route is wechat/ce/<id> (chuansongme proxy). See docs.rsshub.app for more.
settings.browserCookieDescription: Extract cookies from a browser on the machine running the dr-claw service. Make sure Chrome, Arc, Edge, or Firefox on that machine is logged into xiaohongshu.com.
settings.serviceMachineHint: This reads browser cookies from the machine hosting the dr-claw service, not from the device where this page is open. If you are using a remote server, prefer QR login.
settings.cookieSourceDescription: If auto-detect fails, choose the browser that is actually installed on the service machine.
settings.cookieSuccessWithName: Cookie extraction successful! Logged in as {{nickname}}
settings.cookieFailure: Login failed. Make sure you are logged into xiaohongshu.com in your browser.
settings.qrLoginDescription: Recommended for remote deployments and Linux environments where browser cookie extraction may fail.
settings.qrFailure: QR login failed. Please try again.
settings.qrRecommendedHint: This looks like a remote-access setup, so QR login is recommended by default. It does not depend on reading browser cookies from the service machine.
settings.searchKeywordsPlaceholder: 大模型, AI论文, 人工智能...
